finding: Across the last 30 days, roughly 57% of Claude sessions (104 of 181) are meta-tooling — configuring Claude Code, building the self-improvement plugin, managing dotfiles, and exploring Claude Code internals. Product work that directly serves end users (Jhana meditation app, Intender, Autodoro, Whisper Writer, personal assistant) accounts for about 29% (53 sessions). The FOOM goal explicitly connects tooling to capability-building, so this is not off-track per se, but the 2:1 ratio may indicate that building the system is becoming the work rather than a means to do the work. Worth considering whether the tooling investment has reached diminishing returns relative to shipping products.
existing_mitigation: The FOOM goal's connection field ('Multiplying personal capability and automation increases capacity to direct care into the world') explicitly legitimizes tooling work. No mechanism exists to flag when tooling exceeds a healthy ratio.

finding: Three sessions across late March were spent building and running Artcraft, an external 'IDE for artists' project (github.com/storytold/artcraft). The sessions consist entirely of pulling and building — no contributions were made. No stated goal connects to art tooling. This could be evaluating tools for creative expression, helping a friend, or curiosity-driven exploration. The time investment was minimal (3 short sessions) so this is low severity, but if this pattern grows it may signal goal drift.

A single 30-minute session on 2026-03-22 was spent setting up a 'survival corpus for collapse scenarios' project — downloading and organizing reference material. While loosely adjacent to the d/acc goal under Impact & Intellect, it does not clearly connect to any of the specific items listed. This is a one-off and may have been a passing interest; flagging only as data for the cumulative record.
